sp-alternatives.title: Simply Plural Alternatives
sp-alternatives.content:
Replacing Simply Plural: What to Use Instead
There is no perfect one-to-one replacement for Simply Plural. Different apps cover different parts of what it did. The best option depends on what you actually used it for.

At a Glance
  










App
Front
Profiles
Sync
Privacy
Platform






Ampersand
Closest replacement


✓
✓
Cloud
Medium
Mobile




Hivemind
System-focused


✓
✓
Cloud
Medium
Mobile




Ourcana
Beautiful UI


✓
✓
Cloud
Medium
Mobile




Antar
Private, local


~
—
Local
High
Mobile




Anytype
Build your own setup


~
✓
Local-first
Very High
Desktop + Mobile




PluralKit
Best for Discord


✓
✓
Cloud
Medium
Discord



The Reality
Simply Plural did a very specific mix of things:

front tracking
member management
mobile-first design
easy syncing

Most alternatives only do some of those well, not all of them.
So instead of asking “what replaces Simply Plural,” the better question is:
what parts of Simply Plural do you actually need?

If front Tracking is Your Main Need
These are the closest matches:
Ampersand

Very similar conceptually to Simply Plural
Built specifically for systems
Still evolving, but one of the closest replacements right now

Hivemind

Also focused on system tracking
Comparable feature set
Worth trying alongside Ampersand to see which feels better

Ourcana

Another system-focused app
Similar category as the above two
Still developing, so expect some rough edges


If You Care Most about Privacy
Antar

Fully local, no accounts
Very private
Not built specifically for plurality, so you’ll be adapting it

Anytype

Local-first with encrypted sync
Extremely flexible
More of a “build your own system” tool than a ready-made solution

These are good if your main concern is control over your data, not convenience.

If You Use Discord Heavily
PluralKit

Already widely used
Strong for member management and proxying
Not a standalone app, so it doesn’t replace the mobile experience

This works best as part of a setup, not as your only tool.

What You Should Actually Do
Don’t try to pick the “best” app immediately.
Instead:

Figure out what you actually used Simply Plural for

front logging
member profiles
switching history
social features


Try 1–2 options that match that
Give yourself time to adjust

You might end up using:

one app for tracking
another for profiles
PluralKit for Discord

That’s normal for now. At least until someone else integrates similar features.

About Simply Plural
Simply Plural is being discontinued, with servers expected to remain up until at least June 1, 2026. After that, online features will stop working and data will be deleted unless exported.
If you’re still using it, don’t wait too long to start testing alternatives.

Final Note
This space is changing fast right now.
New tools are being built, existing ones are adapting, and things will probably look different in a few months.
You don’t have to get it perfect on the first try. You just need something that works for you.

submission-guidelines.title: Submission Guidelines
submission-guidelines.content:
Submission Guidelines
If you’re submitting something to be listed here, keep it simple.
This site is curated. Not everything gets in.

What You Can Submit

Tools, apps, templates, or guides that are actually useful
Resources people can use, not just look at
Anything you have permission to share

If it doesn’t help someone in a real way, it’s not a fit.

Requirements
Your submission needs to:

Be readable and accessible (skip the aesthetic font spam)
Respect user privacy
Clearly show what’s free vs paid
Actually work

If someone has to fight your setup to use it, it’s getting skipped.

What Doesn’t Fit Here

Conflict-driven content, callouts, or drama-adjacent anything
Tools that break platform rules or put users at risk
Misleading setups (hidden costs, vague access, bait descriptions)
Personal or identifying info left in submissions

If it creates friction, confusion, or problems it’s out.

Notes

Submissions may be edited, declined, or removed without notice
This is a curated resource hub, not a public directory
Paid resources are fine, just label them honestly.

about/index.title: About
about/index.content:
About System Resource Hub
System Resource Hub exists for a pretty simple reason:
finding tools for plural systems shouldn’t be this hard.
Most of what’s out there is scattered, buried, or only shared inside specific spaces. And even when you do find something, it’s hard to tell if it’ll actually work for you.
This site is meant to make that easier.

What This Is

A directory of tools for plural systems
A way to find alternatives without digging for hours
A place that tries to keep things clear and actually usable

It’s not trying to list everything. Just things that are worth knowing about.

Who This Is For

Systems trying to find tools that actually fit
People questioning or figuring things out
Anyone supporting a system and looking for resources


Why Alternatives Matter
No tool works for everyone.
Something that’s perfect for one system might be unusable for another. Needs, preferences, energy levels vary from one person to the next.
So instead of pushing “the best” option, this exists so you can see what’s out there and decide for yourself.

Approach
This site doesn’t filter tools based on morals, drama, or how something was made.
You might care about those things. You might not.
Either way, that choice should be yours, not decided for you ahead of time.
The goal here is just to give you the information in a way that’s actually usable.
For more on how this site approaches inclusion, safety, and resource selection, see the Philosophy page.

How to Use This Site

Browse tools based on what you need
Look at alternatives if something isn’t working
Use energy levels if you’re low and need something easier


How It Grows
This isn’t a finished project.
Things get added, changed, or removed over time. Tools disappear. New ones show up. Stuff improves.
A lot of what ends up here comes from people submitting things or pointing out gaps.

Contribute or Reach Out
If you want to contribute or get in touch:

 Email: systemresourcehub@gmail.com
 Submit a Tool: tally.so/r/9qOBkQ
 Discord: discord.gg/jceEv7s3At

Even small corrections help.

Affiliation
This site isn’t affiliated with any of the tools listed.
Nothing here is an endorsement, just information for your use.

Supporting Developers
If you find something that works well for you, consider supporting the people who made it.
They’re the ones actually building the tools.

Privacy & Intent
This site doesn’t track you or collect personal data beyond what you choose to submit.
There is basic, privacy-friendly analytics (like page visits), but nothing that identifies you.
The point is to provide information, not gather it.
For more details, see the Privacy Policy.

Disclaimer
This isn’t a medical or mental health service.
Nothing here is diagnosis, treatment, or professional advice.
If you need that kind of support, a qualified professional is the right place to go.

🖤
This is a small, intentional project.
It’s built to be useful, respectful, and accessible.
If it helps even a little, then it’s doing its job.
If you want to support the project, you can do that here:
☕ Ko-fi: ko-fi.com/YOURNAME
No pressure. Everything here will always be free.

about/philosophy.title: Philosophy
about/philosophy.content:
Philosophy
Overview
System Resource Hub exists to make finding tools easier.
But more than that, it exists to do it in a way that respects the people using it.
This space is complicated. Needs vary. Experiences vary. What helps one system might not help another.
This site is built with that in mind.

Core Values
Usefulness over Noise
There’s a lot of information out there, but not all of it is usable.
This site focuses on tools that people can realistically use, not just what’s popular, aesthetic, or widely shared.
The goal is to reduce friction, not add to it.

Choice over Curation Bias
This site does not decide what is “best” for you.
Instead of pushing a single option, it shows multiple tools and alternatives so you can decide what fits your needs, energy, and preferences.
Different systems function differently. That matters.

Accessibility Matters
Not everyone has the same:

Energy levels
Cognitive load capacity
Time
Experience

Tools are not one-size-fits-all, and neither is the way people search for them.
This site tries to make discovery easier, especially when energy is low or overwhelm is high.

Non-gatekeeping
This space includes people with different experiences, frameworks, and ways of understanding themselves.
This site does not attempt to define who “counts” or who should or shouldn’t be using these resources.
If something is useful to you, that’s what matters.

Neutral, not Careless
This site does not filter tools based on discourse, personal opinions, or community drama.
However, neutrality does not mean ignoring harm.
If something is clearly unsafe, exploitative, or misleading, it does not belong here.
The goal is to provide options, not to platform harm.

How Resources Are Selected
Tools listed here are chosen based on things like:

Practical usefulness
Clarity of purpose
Accessibility and ease of use
Relevance to plural systems

This is not an exhaustive directory.
It is a curated collection of tools worth knowing about.

What This Site Is Not
System Resource Hub is not:

A medical or mental health service
A diagnostic tool
A replacement for professional care
A social platform

It does not provide treatment, advice, or crisis support.

External Tools
Most tools listed here are created and maintained by third parties.
This means:

Their features can change
Their privacy practices may differ
Their safety and moderation vary

You are encouraged to use your own judgment and review their policies when needed.

Responsibility and Autonomy
This site is designed to give you information, not make decisions for you.
What you choose to use, trust, or engage with is up to you.
That autonomy is intentional.

Closing
This is a small, evolving project.
It is built to be:

Useful
Respectful
Accessible

Not perfect.
If it helps you find something that works, even once, then it’s doing what it was made to do.

about/privacy.title: Privacy Policy
about/privacy.content:
Privacy Policy
1. Overview
System Resource Hub is designed to be a simple, privacy-respecting resource. This Privacy Policy explains what data is (and isn’t) collected when you use the site.
The goal is to provide information, not to collect it.

2. Information We Collect
We collect minimal data, limited to:

Anonymous analytics data (via Umami), such as page views and general device/browser information
Information you voluntarily provide, such as emails, messages, or submitted resources

We do not collect personally identifiable information through tracking, accounts, or hidden data collection.

3. Analytics
This site uses privacy-friendly analytics (Umami) to understand general usage, such as:

Page views
Referring sites
Basic device or browser information

This data is:

Not personally identifiable
Not used to track individual users
Not shared or sold

No cookies are used for analytics.

4. How Information Is Used
Any information collected is used only to:

Maintain and improve the site
Review submitted tools or resources
Respond to messages or inquiries

We do not build user profiles or track behavior across websites.

5. Voluntary Submissions
If you choose to contact or contribute (for example, via email or submission forms), the information you provide may include:

Email address
Message content
Submitted tool or resource details

This information is used only for communication or review purposes and is never sold or shared.

6. External Links
This site links to third-party tools and services.
System Resource Hub is not responsible for the privacy practices of those external sites. You are encouraged to review their policies separately.

7. Data Practices
System Resource Hub does not:

Track users across the web
Build personal profiles
Use invasive tracking methods
Sell or share user data


8. Security
Reasonable measures are taken to protect any information you choose to provide. However, no method of transmission or storage is completely secure, and absolute security cannot be guaranteed.

9. Your Choices
You are not required to provide any personal information to use this site.
If you contact the site and would like your message or information removed, you can request that via email.

10. Contact
If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, you can reach out:

Email: systemresourcehub@gmail.com


11. Updates
This policy may be updated if the site changes in the future. Any updates will be reflected on this page with a revised date.

Posts/20260327-Simply-Plural-Sunset.title: Simply Plural Discontinuation
Posts/20260327-Simply-Plural-Sunset.content:
Simply Plural is Shutting down
TL;DR
Simply Plural is being discontinued. The servers will stay up until at least June 1, 2026, but they will shut down after that. You should start planning to move to another app.

What’s Happening
Simply Plural’s developer has officially announced that development is stopping, and the app will eventually lose server support.
The app will still be available for download, and the servers will stay online for now. The current estimate is that they will remain up until at least June 1, 2026, with a 1–2 month warning before shutdown.
After the servers go down:

The app will only work in offline mode
Some features will stop working entirely
You won’t be able to log in on a new device


What This means for You
If you’re using Simply Plural right now, this is the important part.

Offline mode is not designed for long-term use and may break or behave inconsistently over time
Features that rely on servers, like friends and syncing, will stop working completely
Your data will only be saved locally per device once the servers go dark.

This isn’t something you need to panic about today, but it is something you should start preparing for.

What You Should Do next
You don’t need to rush, but don’t ignore it either.
Start with this:

Export your data from Simply Plural
Look into alternative apps and see what fits your needs
Test other tools before you fully switch

If you wait until the last minute, you’re going to be doing all of this under pressure. It’s a lot easier to do it gradually.

Alternatives
There are already a few tools people are moving to, including:

PluralKit
Ampersand
Ourcana
and others.

I’m putting together a full comparison to help you figure out what actually fits your needs instead of guessing.
Final Note
This situation is still developing. Other tools are adapting, and the community is figuring out where things will settle.
For now, the most important thing is simple:
Make sure your data is safe, and give yourself time to find a replacement that actually works for you.

tools/pluralkit.title: PluralKit
tools/pluralkit.content:
PluralKit
  

A Discord bot that lets systems proxy messages, track fronting, and manage members with distinct identities.
  
  
Type  
Proxying  
  
  
Platform  
Discord  
  
  
License  
  
AGPL  
  
  
  
Cost  
Free  
  
  
Status  
Stable  
  
  


  
Details  
  
  
Privacy  
  
Data stored via bot; visible in Discord where used  
  
  
  
AI-assisted Development  
No  
  
  
Account Required  
  
Discord account required  
  
  
  
  
  
Features  
  
  
Communication  
  
Message proxying (send messages as system members)  
  
  
  
Tracking  
  
Front tracking, member management, system info  
  
  
  
Social  
  
Direct (built for Discord interaction)  
  
  
  
Planning  
None  
  
  
Integrations  
  
Discord (native), works alongside tools like SimplyPlural  
  
  
  
Accessibility  
  
Command-based; may have learning curve
